topic.name: Biodiversity and Species Description
topic.description: The documents provide detailed descriptions of various species, including their taxonomy, habitat, and behavior.

Document 1: The Asian house martin (Delichon dasypus) is a migratory passerine bird of the swallow family Hirundinidae.
This species is locally abundant and is expanding northward in Siberia.
Document 2: The sparsely-spotted stingaree, white-spotted stingaree, or Dixon's stingaree (Urolophus paucimaculatus) is a species of stingray in the family Urolophidae.
This ray is further characterized by a distinctively bell-shaped curtain of skin between the nostrils.
Document 3: Limnoperdon is a fungal genus in the monotypic family Limnoperdaceae.
The genus is also monotypic, as it contains a single species, the aquatic fungus Limnoperdon incarnatum.

topic.name: Naval Warships of World War II

Document 2: Kalinin was a Project 26bis2 Kirov-class cruiser of the Soviet Navy that was built during World War II.

Document 3: Mikasa is a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the Boat Race, an annual rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.

Document 1: The 20th Boat Race between crews from the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge took place on the River Thames on 28 March 1863.
The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course on the River Thames.
Document 3: The 15th Boat Race took place on the River Thames on 27 March 1858.
Typically held annually, the event is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclones in Atlantic
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclones that have impacted regions in the Atlantic, detailing their formation, paths, effects, and historical significance.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Arlene was a rare off-season tropical cyclone that hit Cuba and the Bahamas in May 1981.

Document 2: The effects of Hurricane Katrina in Florida were in both in the southern portion of the state and in the panhandle.

topic.name: Military Conflicts and Sieges
topic.description: This topic encompasses various military engagements and sieges throughout history, highlighting the strategic and operational challenges faced by forces involved.

Document 1: The 40th Infantry Division Slavonska was an infantry formation of the Royal Yugoslav Army during the German-led Axis invasion of the Kingdom of Yugoslavia in April 1941.
The division continued to disintegrate over the following days, then crumbled in the face of the combined arms assault of the 8th Panzer Division.
Document 2: The Battle of Cape Henry was a naval battle in the American War of Independence which took place near the mouth of Chesapeake Bay on 16 March 1781.
Destouches returned to Newport, while Arbuthnot protected the bay for the arrival of additional land troops to reinforce General Arnold.
Document 3: The Siege of Malta was a two-year siege and blockade of the French garrison in Valletta between 1798 and 1800.
Forced to retreat to Valletta, the French garrison faced severe food shortages, exacerbated by the effectiveness of the British blockade.

Most Atlantic tropical storms and hurricanes form between June 1 and November 30.
